blockers.title: Member-facing event URLs are only startsWith-validated and can poison downloaded calendar files
blockers.evidence: `convex/admin/events.ts:272-279` accepts any `meeting_link` that starts with `https://` and is <=500 chars; `setEventLinks` repeats the same weak rule at `convex/admin/events.ts:654-660`. The member page passes that link into `downloadIcs` at `src/portal/views/EventDetailView.tsx:289-298`, and the ICS writer escapes summary/location/description but writes `URL:${event.url}` raw at `src/portal/format.ts:196-213`. A direct admin mutation can store `https://example.com/\r\nATTENDEE:...`, which becomes extra ICS properties in a member download.
blockers.required_fix: Validate all member-facing URLs at the server boundary with a shared parser: reject control characters/whitespace/CRLF, require `https:`, hostname, and bounded length. Escape or safely fold the ICS URL field, and add regression tests for CRLF/control-character URL payloads.

blockers.title: Attendance can be marked and finalized before the event happens, granting standing credit early
blockers.evidence: Spec A3 says `attendance_finalized` closes the event after producer-marked check-in (`specs/panel-experience.spec.md:47-51`), and C9 makes attended events a qualifying standing action (`specs/panel-experience.spec.md:83-89`). `checkIn` only checks event state, not `starts_at`/`ends_at`, before writing `attended` and calling `maybePromoteToActive` (`convex/admin/events.ts:790-833`). `finalizeAttendance` also only checks state before setting `attendance_finalized` (`convex/admin/events.ts:853-860`). Existing tests use events seven days in the future (`tests/convex/events.test.ts:79-89`) and still mark/finalize them (`tests/convex/events.test.ts:823-947`).
blockers.required_fix: Enforce time gates server-side: do not allow `attended` before the event has started, and do not allow finalization before the event has ended. Consider no-show timing explicitly, update UI affordances, and add tests for future check-in/finalization refusal.

non_blocking_notes: `npm test` and `npm run check` could not complete in the read-only sandbox because Vitest/Astro attempted to write generated cache files.
No real secrets were found; secret-scan hits were prior audit text or test placeholders such as `test-secret`.
No diff-added member-facing em dashes were found; the only dash hit was a README table placeholder.

Admin copy says allies have no directory listing (`src/admin/views/shared.ts:291-295`), but the spec and `convex/directory.ts` allow ally listings when otherwise eligible. Fix the copy to avoid operator confusion.

spec_compliance.criterion: A1 Events schema and logistics fields
spec_compliance.evidence: Events and registrations are modeled with the requested state, lane, logistics, capacity, waitlist, link, and check-in fields in `convex/schema.ts` and used by `convex/admin/events.ts`.

spec_compliance.criterion: A2 Member events list/detail, RSVP, waitlist, priority, ICS, pass
spec_compliance.evidence: Lane, RSVP, waitlist, priority, and pass checks are present, but add-to-calendar is not safely delivered because raw server-stored meeting links are inserted into ICS `URL:` lines.

spec_compliance.criterion: A3 Admin event lifecycle, check-in, finalize, materials
spec_compliance.evidence: Admin auth/audit/notification paths exist, but check-in and finalization can happen before the event, breaking the attendance evidence contract.
No host workspace, guest-host, reminder email, event certificate, or contribution-ledger event surface was found in the shipped panel paths.
Board/detail/apply/admin/result paths enforce active lifecycle, lane/audience, deadline, profile-complete, one-application, evergreen, audience immutability, notification, and winner re-check rules.

spec_compliance.criterion: C9 Standing ladder and automatic Active promotion
spec_compliance.evidence: Promotion logic exists, but because future check-in can call `maybePromoteToActive`, the qualifying action is not guaranteed to be an actually attended event.

spec_compliance.criterion: C10 My membership summary and ladder
spec_compliance.evidence: The portal membership view renders lifecycle, number, certificate, standing history, and next-step ladder copy from server state.
`convex/directory.ts` requires active non-minor/non-restricted viewers and listed active_member+ standard/ally rows, returning directory-tier fields only.
Own notification read/mark paths are member-scoped, and reviewed event/opportunity/certificate/standing writes call notification helpers.
Member list omits bulk/export, contact reveal is per-member and audited, status transitions are bounded, and certificate revoke/reissue uses super-admin checks.
Partner create/edit/detail actions are admin-gated, audited, and keep to operational partner records without a corporate-facing surface.
Overview/report queries return queue, KPI, and aggregate data; platform health is super-admin-gated.

spec_compliance.criterion: I19-I20 Workspace shell and preserved protected states
spec_compliance.evidence: Panel shell, cards, tables, filters, modal flows, and protected youth/waiting/held states are present; no live coming-soon surface was found.
Typecheck passed, but full `npm run check`, `npm test`, e2e, and build proof was not available in this read-only review environment.
This review found two ship-blocking defects.

summary: Do not ship. The main authorization, minor-lane, directory, opportunity, contact-reveal, and aggregate-report boundaries look materially sound, but member-facing URL validation is still bypassable and attendance credit can be granted before an event occurs.
